description_long: This object consists of three irregularly shaped stone blocks of varying sizes, clustered together. The largest block is dark gray with visible cracks and subtle patches of moss or lichen, giving it a weathered, urban appearance. The medium block is a lighter gray-green, also showing surface cracks and some discoloration, suggesting exposure to the elements. The smallest block is a simple, rough-edged stone, positioned at the base of the other two. The surfaces of all blocks are matte and rough, with angular facets and chipped edges. The overall grouping suggests use as a physical barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a street or park.
description: A cluster of three weathered, cracked stone blocks in varying shades of gray and green, with rough, angular surfaces and visible signs of aging.
description_view_0: The front view shows the largest dark gray block dominating the foreground, with the medium and small blocks partially visible behind it.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the broad, sloped face of the largest block and reveals more of the medium greenish block behind it.
description_view_2: From this angle, the cracks and surface details of both the largest and medium blocks are prominent, with the smallest block less visible.
description_view_3: The rear view displays all three blocks clearly, showing their relative sizes and the rough, chipped edges of each stone.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or placed directly on the ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface cracks, chipped edges, and patches of moss or lichen indicate prolonged exposure to weather and urban conditions.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: clustered together as an isolated group
